mcqs.question: Which of the following is the primary purpose of an I/O interface in a computer system?

options.text: To synchronize data transfer speed between the CPU and peripheral devices
To increase the clock speed of the CPU
To provide additional cache memory
To replace the function of the main memory

mcqs.explanation: I/O interfaces resolve the speed mismatch between the fast CPU and slower peripheral devices, managing data transfer synchronization.

mcqs.question: In the context of I/O communication, what is the fundamental characteristic of a memory-mapped I/O system?

options.text: I/O devices and memory share the same address space
I/O devices have a completely separate address space from memory
It requires a special I/O instruction set for access
It is slower than port-mapped I/O due to address decoding

mcqs.explanation: Memory-mapped I/O uses the same address bus to access both memory and I/O devices, meaning portions of the memory address space are assigned to I/O registers.

mcqs.question: What is a major disadvantage of Programmed I/O (PIO) when compared to interrupt-driven I/O?

options.text: The CPU wastes cycles polling device status instead of doing useful work
It requires much more complex hardware to implement
It cannot be used to transfer data to memory
It is inherently less secure than other methods

mcqs.explanation: In PIO, the CPU must repeatedly check (poll) the status of the I/O device, which is an inefficient use of processing time, known as 'busy-waiting'.

mcqs.question: During an interrupt service routine (ISR), why is it often necessary to save the contents of certain CPU registers?

options.text: To preserve the state of the interrupted program so it can resume correctly
To free up space in the CPU cache for the ISR
To allow the ISR to modify the registers without affecting the main program
To prevent the ISR from accessing sensitive system data

mcqs.explanation: Saving the CPU state (registers, program counter, etc.) ensures that after the ISR completes, the CPU can restore the state and continue executing the interrupted program exactly where it left off.

mcqs.question: What is the primary advantage of using Direct Memory Access (DMA) for data transfer?

options.text: It reduces CPU overhead by allowing data transfer without continuous CPU intervention
It increases the speed of the CPU
It eliminates the need for an interrupt controller
It allows the CPU to directly control the data transfer process

mcqs.explanation: DMA allows the transfer of large blocks of data directly between memory and I/O devices with minimal CPU involvement, freeing the CPU to perform other tasks.
